export_view.value:
RepairQ includes a powerful Business Intelligence (BI) Suite, integrated with Looker. This tool provides a set of visual dashboards covering key performance indicators (KPIs) essential for managing a retail repair business. These reports offer quick insights into sales trends, inventory levels, and repair progress, helping you make more informed decisions.

BI Dashboards are available to Admin users on our Advanced and Professional plans. Users on the Advanced Plan can view a wide range of pre-built reports and dashboards, giving them access to critical business insights. However, they are limited to viewing pre-made reports and cannot create new ones. Professional Plan users have the additional capability to build custom reports, allowing them to tailor analytics to their specific needs and uncover deeper insights.

In this article, we will demonstrate how to create and save a custom report in Looker.

Additional Resources

  • Business Intelligence (BI) Resources

  • Business Intelligence - Dashboards

  • Business Intelligence Dashboard - Explore

  • Business Intelligence - Inventory Forecasting

  • Business Intelligence - Troubleshooting

Access the Business Intelligence (BI) Report Builder

From the Navigation Bar, go to Reports → Analytics. When viewing your BI dashboard, click Report Builder. The Report Builder dropdown offers a range of categories, including Accounting, Billing Agent, Customer, Ticket, and more. When building your report, select the category most closely related to the type of data you want to analyze. For example, if you're creating a report to track Ticket data, choose the Ticket category. This ensures that you’re pulling the most relevant data for your report.

Assign Report Fields

When building a report, you’ll see fields organized by category in the left column of the Report Builder. These fields are directly linked to data from your environment. Select fields from the categories that correspond to the information you want to include in your report. You can also use the keyword search feature to quickly find specific fields, which will be displayed under their respective categories.

For this example, we will add the following fields to the report: Ticket ID, Customer First Name, Customer Last Name, Ticket Status Updated Date, Ticket Status, and Assigned Ticket Resolution Name.

Note: To remove a field, select the field again, or navigate to the ‘In Use’ tab to view all currently selected fields. From there, click on any unwanted fields to remove them from your report.

Run the Report and Refine Results with Filters

Once you've selected the desired fields, click the Run button to generate your report and view the results based on your selections. To refine these results further, you can add filters by clicking the Gear Icon next to the field you wish to filter. From the list of available actions, select Filter to set your desired criteria.

Selected filters will appear at the top of the report, allowing you to customize your criteria. For example, the ‘Ticket Status Updated Date' filter can be adjusted to show results from ‘In the last number of days’, a specific date range, or before a certain date. This flexibility enables you to tailor the report to your specific needs. Once you have set your filters, click Run to refine the results.

In this example, we’ve added filters for ‘Ticket Status Updated Date’ and ‘Ticket Resolution Name.’

Note: The Ticket IDs are linked to your tickets in RepairQ. Clicking one will open the ticket in a new tab.

Saving Reports

To save a report, click the Gear Icon at the top-right corner of the page. From the list of actions, select Save → As a new dashboard, and then add a title and choose the destination for the report. We recommend saving your report under My Folder as a dedicated location for any user-generated reports. This keeps your reports organized and easily accessible for future use.

Access Saved Reports

When viewing the analytics snapshot, click the Folder Icon in the upper-right corner of the dashboard. This will expand the sidebar, allowing you to select My Folder and access your saved dashboards.

To expand a Look, click the Compass Icon and select Explore from Here to dive deeper into the report and explore the underlying data.

Note: Click the Folder icon to expand/hide the folder sidebar.

Automated Report Scheduling

When viewing the Report Dashboard, click the Options button. From the list of available actions, select Schedule Delivery to set up automated report delivery.

Give your schedule a name, choose the recurrence period, specify the destination email(s), and select the format in which you'd like the report to be delivered. Once you've configured these settings, click Save to finalize the schedule.

START 1.21.X
